title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  7  - 2017: Google Earthing Rome

enrichment.summary: Kyle is recreating ancient Rome in Google Earth, building 3D structures like the Colosseum, Pantheon, and Temple of Claudius by stacking color-coded height layers. He reflects on the uncanny emotional resonance of virtually inhabiting Roman viewsheds that millions of historical people shared. He also mentions hovering at the edge of financial trouble while living in Boulder.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: Google Earth as a reconstruction tool
stances.position: capable enough to produce convincing ancient Rome if you work within its height-layer constraints
deeply moving — taps into a collective historical psyche shared by millions over centuries
precarious but hopeful he can monetize the project before going into credit card debt

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't remember the last time I had a video 5 days or something.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think back in those days, I was doing like, I had the idea of doing a region a week.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After that, I decided to do a region a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I broke the work up into all the different regions, the different files.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on them individually at the bench a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I saved them together as one file again just so I could look at it all the way.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: They're all kind of more or less at the same level, though some work better.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Right now I'm attaching colors and doing the finishing touches on all the buildings, making them look realistic.

claims.subject: rotary barn
claims.claim: When you type rotary barn on YouTube, you see flyovers of ancient Rome.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Google Earth can't have blocks of color start to heighten and then edit another height and taper it with the heights up and down perfectly.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: You're limited because Google Earth follows the contour of the earth.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: You can't actually do enough with Google Earth.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: I was building the Colosseum and it has a modern Colosseum going around it.

claims.subject: Colosseum
claims.claim: I'm traveling through the Colosseum on the third level, turning and looking at the view.

claims.subject: statue
claims.claim: I built a statue today by making levels that hover at a level in meter increments.

claims.subject: statue
claims.claim: If you look at it from afar and make it solid, it looks like one solid mass.

claims.subject: dome of the pantheon
claims.claim: I did the dome of the pantheon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm too lazy to do the screen capture.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm really not that far away from making it look really good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Every day it's much easier to do building.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm still hovering above surface level financially.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Hopefully I'll get next or make some money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't go into credit card debt for a bit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can see myself getting to a point where I want to get first Google for them and then when you see the videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: At that point, I'll probably go to polish my website somewhere on the surface.

claims.subject: Temple of Claudius
claims.claim: Today I was exploring around buildings, coming out the front end of the Temple of Claudius.

claims.subject: Temple of Claudius
claims.claim: The view looks like a familiar, beautiful site that the Romans had.

claims.subject: Romans
claims.claim: The view is one that everybody has had for hundreds of years, thousands of people, millions of people.

claims.subject: public mind psyche
claims.claim: The view has been in the public mind psyche, the caches, records, that mind.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: When you're looking at the view of a guy on Google Earth, you're hooked into it and you're going, oh my god.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to travel around the entire city of Rome like I live there.

claims.subject: Temple of Claudius
claims.claim: Next to the Temple of Claudius, there's a little physical track that doesn't show up in any of the other maps.

claims.subject: track
claims.claim: The name of the track was like a little Gallica, it didn't show up on the maps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I'm going to go look at that tomorrow.
